changes.finding: CONFIRMED: cbc system present at scoring_policy.yaml:47-50. system_weight: 0.1, min_biomarkers_required: 2, biomarkers: ['hemoglobin', 'hematocrit', 'white_blood_cells', 'platelets']. Iron, ferritin, transferrin, RBC indices (mcv, mch, mchc, rdw) have NO scoring bands in scoring_policy.yaml. These markers will produce unscored_reason_missing_lab_reference_range via the cbc rail. This is expected behaviour. STOP GATE applies: if implementation requires creating new scoring bands for these markers, Cursor must stop and report the blocker per prompt §Scoring and policy rules.

CONFIRMED: domain_score_assembler.py currently wires 4 domains (lines 566-858): wave1_cardiovascular, wave1_blood_sugar, wave1_liver, wave1_kidney. No wave1_blood_iron_oxygen block exists. No _is_wave1_blood_iron_oxygen predicate exists. _WAVE1_PLAIN_DESCRIPTOR at line 371 has 4 entries (no blood/iron/oxygen). Pattern to follow for new domain: _is_wave1_kidney at line 199 uses signal_id allowlist (not system-field filtering).

CONFIRMED: _WAVE1_DOMAIN_SUBSYSTEM_ORDER at wave1_subsystem_evidence.py:20-37 has 4 entries (cv, blood_sugar, liver, kidney). WAVE1_DOMAIN_IDS frozenset at line 39 auto-derived from the dict. No blood/iron/oxygen entry exists. Sprint must add wave1_blood_iron_oxygen key with subsystem ID tuple.

CONFIRMED: WAVE1_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS frozenset at health_system_card_evidence.py:26-37 has 8 subsystem IDs (met x2, cv x3, liv x2, ren x1). No blood/iron/oxygen subsystem ID. Sprint must add new ID(s) here. PILOT_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS at line 50 is aliased to WAVE1_COMPILED_SUBSYSTEM_IDS — updating the frozenset updates both.

CONFIRMED: estate_index_v1.yaml has 8 card_evidence_artefacts (none blood/iron/oxygen). Sprint must append a new entry for the blood/iron/oxygen subsystem compiled card + manifest. Must follow existing schema (subsystem_id, artefact_path, compile_manifest_path, launch_classification: launch_included_compiled).

CONFIRMED from P1-1 §5.2: In-scope biomarkers confirmed in backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml — haemoglobin (~733), hematocrit (~749), rbc (~917), mcv (~936), mch (~958), mchc (~975), rdw_cv (~992), rdw_sd (~1012), ferritin (~586), iron (~1824), transferrin (~1844), transferrin_saturation (~1865), vitamin_b12 and folate (yes). ABSENT from SSOT and packages: tibc and uibc — must not use. Ferritin is tagged 'immune' system in SSOT — cross-system routing risk (see stage_2b check below).

CONFIRMED REGRESSION RISK: test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py:225-228 asserts exact set WAVE1_DOMAIN_IDS == {'wave1_cardiovascular', 'wave1_blood_sugar', 'wave1_liver', 'wave1_kidney'}. After P1-3 adds wave1_blood_iron_oxygen to wave1_subsystem_evidence.py, this assertion WILL fail unless Cursor updates the test to include 'wave1_blood_iron_oxygen' in the expected set. This is a required, expected test update — not a pre-existing failure.

Ferritin is tagged 'immune' system in backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml (~586), not 'cbc'. Any _is_wave1_blood_iron_oxygen signal predicate that filters by system == 'cbc' will silently exclude ferritin signals. Pattern to follow: _is_wave1_kidney at domain_score_assembler.py:199-210 uses explicit signal_id allowlist ('signal_egfr_low', 'signal_creatinine_high', 'signal_creatinine_low'). The blood/iron/oxygen signal predicate must use signal_id allowlist logic, not system-field matching.

The cbc scoring rail includes white_blood_cells and platelets (scoring_policy.yaml:50). The _is_wave1_blood_iron_oxygen signal predicate must NOT use a broad cbc-system match that inadvertently pulls WBC or platelet signals into the blood/iron domain. Explicit signal_id allowlist required, listing only governed blood/iron/oxygen signal IDs.

B12 and folate fall under 'nutritional' scoring system (scoring_policy.yaml:37-38: system_weight: 0.0, biomarkers: []). If B12/folate are included in the blood/iron/oxygen domain (permitted by P1-1 §5.2 for MCV/macrocytosis context), they cannot contribute scored output via the nutritional rail. They may appear as subsystem evidence markers only if the compiled card evidence supports it. Do not create scored B12/folate bands under cbc or any other system.

P1-1 §5.3 CONFIRMED: signal_iron_deficiency_context, signal_iron_overload_context, signal_hemoglobin_low, signal_mcv_high are blocked_pending_frame_adjudication. signal_ferritin_high/signal_ferritin_low are runtime_loaded with mixed promotion safety and medical review required. CBC tranche blocker report (KB-S57_CBC_TRANCHE_BLOCKER_REPORT.md) documents unresolved batch governance. Cursor must read this report before deciding which signals to wire. Do NOT activate any blocked signal. Only use signals confirmed runtime_active_canonical or runtime_loaded without adjudication blocks.

cbc system scoring bands in scoring_policy.yaml cover only hemoglobin and hematocrit per §§235-248. Iron, ferritin, transferrin, mcv, mch, mchc, rdw have no bands. If domain implementation requires scoring these markers beyond their existing lab-range comparison, new bands would be required. Per prompt §Scoring and policy rules: 'if scoring-policy ambiguity is significant, stop and report the blocker.' Do not improvise new bands.

CONFIRMED: tibc and uibc are ABSENT from backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml and have no KB packages per P1-1 §5.2 and §5.7. Do not use TIBC or UIBC. Prompt explicitly: 'TIBC / UIBC only if present and governed' — they are not present.

Real implementation work exists.

overall_verdict: HARDENED — All five front matter fields confirmed (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:2-6). Prerequisites P1-1 and P1-2 on main confirmed. All authority paths verified to exist. Key source content read and cross-referenced: scoring_policy.yaml cbc system confirmed (hemoglobin/hematocrit/wbc/platelets only — no iron/ferritin/transferrin bands); domain_score_assembler.py confirmed 4-domain-only with no blood/iron/oxygen block; wave1_subsystem_evidence.py confirmed 4-domain-only; health_system_card_evidence.py confirmed 8-subsystem frozenset with no blood/iron entry; estate_index_v1.yaml confirmed 8 compiled cards, none blood/iron/oxygen. Four hardening-elevated risks: (1) test_domain_ux1c_governed_subsystem_evidence.py:225-228 WAVE1_DOMAIN_IDS set assertion will fail — mandatory test update; (2) ferritin cross-system routing risk (immune tag vs cbc rail) — _is_wave1_blood_iron_oxygen must use signal_id allowlist not system-field match; (3) WBC/platelet leakage risk — same allowlist requirement; (4) B12/folate cannot contribute scoring via nutritional rail (weight 0.0). Two STOP GATES confirmed: (1) blocked signal activation prohibited — must read KB-S57 blocker report first; (2) new scoring bands for iron/ferritin/transferrin prohibited unless existing structure clearly supports. Cursor may proceed under TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH governance.
